title: What to Actually Include in Your Board Deck Financials
excerpt: The instinct when building board financials is to include more. In practice, it usually signals the opposite. Here's what actually belongs and how to structure it.

title: Rolling Forecast vs Annual Budget for SaaS: Which One to Use
excerpt: The rolling forecast versus annual budget debate usually ends the same way: you need both. They do different things, and here's how to use each one properly.

title: Budget vs Actuals: Building a Variance Analysis That Gets Used
excerpt: Most variance analysis reports get skimmed and filed. The ones that actually drive decisions have a few things in common — and format is the least of them.

title: How to Present Your Budget to the Board
excerpt: Most budget presentations fail before they start. The finance team spends weeks building the model and then hands the board a spreadsheet walkthrough. Here's how to approach it differently.

title: The Real Limitations of AI in Financial Planning
excerpt: The honest conversation about AI in finance is harder to find than the promotional one. Here's a practitioner's take on where the real limits are — including the risk of cognitive surrender.

title: FP&A Software Compared: Mosaic, Cube, Pigment, and the Rest
excerpt: The tool that's right for a 30-person Series A is almost certainly wrong for a 300-person Series C. Here's how to think through the FP&A software decision.

title: ChatGPT for FP&A: Practical Use Cases That Hold Up
excerpt: Most lists of AI use cases for finance are too vague to act on. Here are the use cases that actually hold up under production conditions, and an honest note on where the limits are.

title: AI for FP&A: What Actually Works Right Now
excerpt: Every FP&A team is somewhere on the AI adoption curve. Here's a practical framework: people, process, data, and tools — and why leadership has to come first.

title: What Fractional FP&A Actually Costs in 2026
excerpt: Pricing for fractional FP&A is one of those topics that's hard to find straight answers on. Here's what the market actually looks like — hourly rates, monthly retainers, and how it compares to a full-time hire.

title: Fractional CFO vs FP&A: They Are Not the Same Role
excerpt: Founders use these terms interchangeably, but they are different things. Here's what each role actually covers and how to know which one you actually need.

title: Fractional FP&A vs Full-Time Hire: How to Decide
excerpt: Either can work. What determines the right call is usually the candidate pool and what the role actually needs to be — and there's a pattern worth understanding before you start interviewing.

title: When to Hire a Fractional CFO
excerpt: Most founders wait too long to bring in senior finance support. Here's how to know when a fractional CFO is the right move — and when fractional FP&A might be enough.

title: AI in the Month-End Close: Where It Actually Helps
excerpt: AI can genuinely save time in the month-end close — but only in the right places. Here's where it earns its keep, where it doesn't, and one concrete workflow worth trying.

title: How to Build Your First AI Agent in FP&A (Without Overbuilding It)
excerpt: Most FP&A teams that struggle with AI agents don't fail on the technology. They fail because they try to automate the wrong thing, or they overbuild before they've proven anything works. Here's a practical sequence for getting your first agent off the ground.

title: AI Didn't Take the FP&A Job. It Took the Part Nobody Wanted Anyway.
excerpt: AI is automating the analysis work in FP&A, but the part that actually drives value, owning a recommendation, pushing back on a sandbagged number, making a CFO confident enough to act, was never something a model could do. Here's what that means for how finance professionals should be spending their time.

title: How Adobe's CFO Is Using Agentic AI to Redefine Finance (And What FP&A Teams Can Learn)
excerpt: Adobe's CFO Dan Durn has turned the finance org into an internal AI lab, using agentic AI to automate complex workflows. Here's what smaller FP&A teams can take from that playbook.

title: Headcount Planning at Scale: What Changes When You Hit 200 Employees
excerpt: Below 200 employees, headcount planning is basically a list. Above 200, the whole job changes — and most FP&A teams don't see the shift coming until they're already behind.

title: Reasonable vs Defensible vs Probability-Weighted Forecasts
excerpt: A forecast that looks reasonable is only the starting point. Strong FP&A teams push further by making assumptions defensible and ultimately building probability-weighted scenarios that show leadership the real range of outcomes.

title: Six Things McKinsey Learned from a Year of Agentic AI (That Matter for FP&A)
excerpt: McKinsey analyzed 50+ agentic AI builds and distilled six lessons. Here's the FP&A-specific read on each one.
